by
  have h₀ : ∃ (C : Set (Fin 3 → ℝ)) (hC : Convex ℝ C) (x : ℕ → Fin 3 → ℝ) (y : Set ℕ → Fin 3 → ℝ),
      ∀ i s, x i + y s ∈ C ↔ i ∈ s := by
    use {f : Fin 3 → ℝ | f 0 = 0}
    -- Prove that the set {f : Fin 3 → ℝ | f 0 = 0} is convex.
    have hC : Convex ℝ {f : Fin 3 → ℝ | f 0 = 0} := by
      refine' convex_subtype_val _
      <;>
      (try norm_num) <;>
      (try simp [Fin.sum_univ_succ]) <;>
      (try
        {
          -- Prove that the set is convex by showing that for any two functions f, g in the set and any scalar t in [0,1],
          -- the linear combination t*f + (1-t)*g is also in the set.
          intros f g hf hg t ht
          simp only [Fin.forall_fin_succ] at hf hg ⊢
          simp [Fin.sum_univ_succ, hf, hg]
          <;>
          (try norm_num) <;>
          (try linarith [ht.1, ht.2]) <;>
          (try ring_nf) <;>
          (try simp_all) <;>
          (try nlinarith [ht.1, ht.2])
        }) <;>
      (try
        {
          -- Handle the case when t is a scalar
          intros t f hf
          simp only [Fin.forall_fin_succ] at hf ⊢
          simp [Fin.sum_univ_succ, hf]
          <;>
          (try norm_num) <;>
          (try linarith [ht.1, ht.2]) <;>
          (try ring_nf) <;>
          (try simp_all) <;>
          (try nlinarith [ht.1, ht.2])
        })
    -- Define the functions x and y
    use hC
    use fun i => ![1, (i : ℝ), 0]
    use fun s => ![0, 0, if (0 : ℕ) ∈ s then 1 else 0]
    intro i s
    -- Prove that x i + y s ∈ C if and only if i ∈ s
    simp [Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_succ, Matrix.head_cons, Fin.forall_fin_succ]
    <;>
    (try
      {
        by_cases h : (0 : ℕ) ∈ s <;>
        simp [h] <;>
        (try { norm_num <;>
          (try { aesop }) }) <;>
        (try {
          norm_num at * <;>
          (try { aesop }) <;>
          (try { linarith }) <;>
          (try { ring_nf at * <;> aesop })
        })
      }) <;>
    (try
      {
        norm_num at * <;>
        (try { aesop }) <;>
        (try { linarith }) <;>
        (try { ring_nf at * <;> aesop })
      }) <;>
    (try { aesop }) <;>
    (try { linarith }) <;>
    (try { ring_nf at * <;> aesop })
  -- Use the result from h₀ to conclude the proof
  exact h₀

  <;>
  aesop

by
  use {f : Fin 3 → ℝ | f 0 ≥ 0}
  use convex_of_mem_or (by
    intro x hx y hy t ht
    cases' hx with hx hx <;> cases' hy with hy hy <;>
      (try { contradiction }) <;>
      (try {
        simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Fin.forall_fin_succ]
        <;>
        ((try { nlinarith [ht.1, ht.2] })) <;>
        ((try {
          refine' Or.inl _
          nlinarith [ht.1, ht.2]
        })) <;>
        ((try {
          refine' Or.inr _
          nlinarith [ht.1, ht.2]
        }))
      }) <;>
      (try {
        simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Fin.forall_fin_succ]
        <;>
        (try { nlinarith [ht.1, ht.2] }) <;>
        (try {
          refine' Or.inl _
          nlinarith [ht.1, ht.2]
        }) <;>
        (try {
          refine' Or.inr _
          nlinarith [ht.1, ht.2]
        })
      })) (by
    intro h
    simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
    <;>
    (try { nlinarith })
    <;>
    (try {
      refine' ⟨0, _⟩
      norm_num
    }))
  use fun i => ![0, 0, (i : ℝ)]
  use fun s => ![0, 0, (if s = ∅ then 0 else 1 : ℝ)]
  intro i s
  simp [Fin.sum_univ_succ]
  <;>
  by_cases h : s = ∅ <;>
  simp_all [h, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one, Fin.val_two]
  <;>
  (try { norm_num }) <;>
  (try { constructor <;> intro h' <;> simp_all [h'] <;>
    (try { nlinarith }) <;>
    (try {
      cases' s with s <;> simp_all (config := {decide := true})
      <;> aesop
    }) }) <;>
  (try { aesop })

by
  use {f : Fin 3 → ℝ | f 0 = 1}
  use by
    -- Prove that the set {f : Fin 3 → ℝ | f 0 = 1} is convex
    refine' convex_singleton _
  use fun i _ => 0
  use fun s : Set ℕ => fun j => if j = 0 ∧ (0 : ℕ) ∈ s then 1 else 0
  intro i s
  -- Simplify the membership condition using the definitions of x and y
  simp [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
  <;>
  (try decide) <;>
  (try
    {
      by_cases h : i = 0 <;>
      by_cases h' : (0 : ℕ) ∈ s <;>
      simp_all (config := {decide := true})
      <;>
      (try contradiction) <;>
      (try linarith)
    }) <;>
  (try aesop)

by
  have h_main : ∃ (C : Set (Fin 3 → ℝ)) (hC : Convex ℝ C) (x : ℕ → Fin 3 → ℝ) (y : Set ℕ → Fin 3 → ℝ), ∀ i s, x i + y s ∈ C ↔ i ∈ s := by
    use {f : Fin 3 → ℝ | f 0 ≥ 0}, convex_const
    use fun i j => if j = 0 then 0 else 0
    use fun s j => if j = 0 then if i ∈ s then 1 else -1 else 0
    intro i s
    simp [Fin.forall_fin_succ]
    <;>
    (try { aesop }) <;>
    (try {
      by_cases h : i ∈ s <;>
      simp_all [h] <;>
      norm_num <;>
      (try { linarith }) <;>
      (try { aesop })
    }) <;>
    (try {
      cases i <;>
      simp_all (config := {decide := true})
      <;>
      norm_num <;>
      (try { linarith }) <;>
      (try { aesop })
    }) <;>
    (try {
      aesop
    })
  exact h_main

by
  classical
  use {f : Fin 3 → ℝ | f 0 = 0}
  use convex_subset (convex_prod (convex_const _) (convex_const _)) (fun p q h => by
    simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Fin.forall_fin_succ, Fin.val_zero] at h ⊢
    aesop)
  use fun i => fun _ => (1 : ℝ)
  use fun s => fun j => if (0 : ℕ) ∈ s then (0 : ℝ) else (if (j : ℕ) = 0 then -1 else 0)
  intro i s
  simp [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
  <;>
  (try
    {
      by_cases h : (0 : ℕ) ∈ s <;>
      simp_all [h] <;>
      (try { aesop }) <;>
      (try { norm_num }) <;>
      (try {
        by_cases h₁ : i = 0 <;>
        simp_all [h₁] <;>
        aesop
      })
    }) <;>
  (try {
    by_cases h : i = 0 <;>
    simp_all [h] <;>
    aesop
  }) <;>
  aesop
